mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate over the trailing 3 months lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically raising present-value valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of the surrounding macro regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and forward 20d SPX returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window, or the sign of the relationship flips in more than one of three consecutive 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises 0.3 percentage points or more above its trailing 12-month low, a Sahm-rule-like recession signal activates, foreshadowing weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings that depress S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=3.8 near multi-year lows at snapshot
regime_hint: labor market turning point / early recession onset
failure_condition: In more than 50% of historical trigger episodes since 1970, 60-day forward SPX returns following the trigger are positive, or the average post-trigger return is not significantly below the unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Elevated nominal FEDFUNDS combined with decelerating CPIAUCSL inflation implies a rising real policy rate, tightening financial conditions faster than headline nominal rates suggest and suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days; the effect requires both series to move jointly since either alone is insufficient to signal real-rate tightening.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the implied real-rate proxy versus forward 20d returns is indistinguishable from the univariate FEDFUNDS-only signal (difference < 0.02), indicating no incremental interaction value, or real-rate spikes are not followed by below-median returns in at least 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS elevated and rising), a sustained decline in HOUST housing starts signals interest-rate-sensitive demand destruction that precedes broader economic slowdown, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter; outside tightening regimes the same HOUST decline carries no reliable predictive signal.
failure_condition: HOUST 3-month declines during periods when FEDFUNDS is above its 2-year moving average fail to precede below-median 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of episodes, or the relationship is equally strong in easing regimes, invalidating the regime-conditionality claim.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month momentum in INDPRO industrial production reflects expanding real output and corporate revenue capacity, driving a short-horizon positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets incrementally price in improved earnings prospects.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO monthly change and forward 5d SPX returns stays below 0.02 across all rolling 2-year windows, indicating the momentum signal carries no short-horizon predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth decelerates below 1%, a labor-market slowdown signal activates, indicating weakening income growth and consumer spending capacity that depresses S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as earnings expectations are revised down.
failure_condition: Following trigger episodes, 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of cases, or average returns post-trigger do not differ significantly (p > 0.05) from unconditional average returns.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in RSAFS retail sales growth and a rise in UNRATE jointly signal an emerging consumption-driven demand contraction not captured by either series alone, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week as markets react to the compounded weak-demand signal.
failure_condition: The joint RSAFS-UNRATE trigger produces forward 5d returns statistically indistinguishable from either single-series trigger in isolation (interaction effect size < 0.01 in Rank IC terms), or triggered episodes show positive average returns.

mechanism_narrative: In a disinflation regime where CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth is steadily declining toward the Federal Reserve's target, markets anticipate an eventual pivot to rate cuts, and this expectation supports elevated S&P 500 valuations, generating positive forward returns over the next quarter; outside disinflation regimes (stable or rising inflation) this mechanism does not apply.
failure_condition: During periods of confirmed CPIAUCSL YoY deceleration for 6+ consecutive months, 60-day forward SPX returns are not significantly higher than the unconditional historical average (p > 0.05), or returns are negative in more than 45% of such episodes.
